reasoning: The Lean statement matches the informal statement clause-by-clause: complete metric space ([MetricSpace X] [CompleteSpace X]), closed (IsClosed), nonempty (.Nonempty), bounded (Bornology.IsBounded), nested (E n ⊇ E (n+1)), diameter tends to 0 (Tendsto (Metric.diam (E n)) atTop (𝓝 0)), and the conclusion that the intersection is a singleton (∃ a, Set.iInter E = {a}), which is exactly "consists of exactly one point". The index shift (ℕ starting at 0 vs. the informal starting at 1) is immaterial because the decreasing-nested hypothesis makes the two intersections equal. All types and quantifier structure line up, so this is a strictly faithful formalization.
